text: Cheating and dishonestly inducing delivery of property â€” Whoever cheats and thereby dishonestly induces the person deceived to deliver any property shall be punished with imprisonment up to seven years and fine.
Punishment for criminal breach of trust. Whoever commits criminal breach of trust shall be punished with imprisonment up to three years or fine or both.
Assault or criminal force to woman with intent to outrage her modesty shall be punishable with imprisonment from one year up to five years and fine.
Cheating. Whoever deceives another person and dishonestly induces them to deliver property or do something causing harm is said to cheat.
Punishment for identity theft. Fraudulent use of another person's password or digital signature is punishable with imprisonment up to three years and fine.
Cheating by personation using computer resources. Impersonating another person online to cheat is punishable with imprisonment up to three years and fine.
Word or gesture intended to insult the modesty of a woman shall be punishable with imprisonment up to three years and fine.
Sexual harassment includes unwelcome physical contact, demands for sexual favors, showing pornography without consent, or sexually colored remarks.
A consumer or group of consumers may file a complaint before a consumer dispute redressal commission regarding defective goods or deficient services.
Criminal intimidation. Whoever threatens another person causing fear of injury to body, reputation, or property shall be punished with imprisonment or fine.
Forgery for purpose of cheating shall be punishable with imprisonment up to seven years and fine.
Punishment for theft. Whoever commits theft shall be punished with imprisonment up to three years or fine or both.
Punishment for extortion. Whoever intentionally puts a person in fear of injury to obtain property commits extortion.
Punishment for robbery. Whoever commits robbery shall be punished with rigorous imprisonment up to ten years and fine.
Punishment for rape includes rigorous imprisonment not less than ten years which may extend to life imprisonment.
Cruelty by husband or relatives of husband towards a married woman is punishable with imprisonment up to three years and fine.
Stalking. Repeatedly following or contacting a woman despite clear disinterest constitutes stalking.
Computer related offences such as unauthorized access, data theft, or introducing virus are punishable with imprisonment up to three years.
Publishing or transmitting obscene material in electronic form is punishable with imprisonment and fine.
Breach of confidentiality and privacy of electronic records is punishable with imprisonment or fine.
Dangerous driving that endangers public safety is punishable with imprisonment or fine.
Driving under the influence of alcohol or drugs is punishable with imprisonment or fine.
Defines rights of consumers including right to safety, right to information, and right to seek redressal.
Consumers may file complaints regarding defective goods or deficient services before the consumer commission.
Sale of immovable property refers to transfer of ownership in exchange for a price.
Lease of property refers to transfer of the right to enjoy property for a specified time in exchange for rent.
Divorce may be granted on grounds such as cruelty, desertion, or conversion.
Daughters have equal rights as sons in ancestral property.
Employers must pay workers wages not less than the minimum rate fixed by law.
Dismissal or termination disputes between employer and worker are treated as industrial disputes.
Every child aged six to fourteen has the right to free and compulsory education.
Private schools must reserve 25 percent seats for economically weaker sections.
Persons whose income exceeds the taxable limit must file income tax returns.
Goods and Services Tax shall be levied on supply of goods and services.
Children or relatives must maintain parents or senior citizens unable to maintain themselves.
Every person has the right to access affordable and quality mental healthcare services.
All clinical establishments must be registered and follow government standards.
